steps.title: Identify the two independent clauses
steps.content: Before the blank: Nakalembe doesn't just compile the information — a complete clause. After the blank: she also shares her findings with African farmers... — another complete clause. The conjunctive adverb though connects the two.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation pattern
steps.content:
Per the answer key, the right pattern places a comma before though and a semicolon after it to join the second clause: information, though;.

Choice B (comma after though) creates a comma splice between two complete clauses; choice C reverses the order with a semicolon before; choice D omits the joining punctuation, producing a run-on.

The answer is A.
